purpose: Determine which of the program's ~50-70 cumulative claims represent genuine phenomena vs. statistical artifacts from repeated testing on the same data
components: Bonferroni/HLZ adjustment for correlated tests requiring t≥3.8
Systematic review of iter_0006-0009 testable claims
Classification of survivors: UIP failure (t>5), carry fat tails (t>10), regime-conditional rate-FX (F=7.2), PCA collapse (mechanically verifiable)
Identification of non-survivors with specific test statistics
current_assessment: The single most important meta-finding: the program has identified a small number of genuine phenomena surrounded by a much larger body of suggestive-but-inconclusive findings. Only 3-4 claims survive the threshold.
limitations: Appropriate correlation structure for test adjustment is debatable
Some claims may be borderline and sensitive to threshold choice
New data could promote moderate-confidence findings above threshold
